prerequisites: A Picqer account with access to the web app. Your account has a unique subdomain (for example example.picqer.com); the subdomain is the organization_name used to reach https://{organization_name}.picqer.com/api/v1/.
An API key created in the Picqer web app under Settings > API Keys. The API key serves as the username for the connection.
A start_date to bound the initial sync, supplied as the start_date config field.
All API access occurs over HTTPS; Picqer responds with JSON.

authMethods.name: API key via HTTP Basic Auth
authMethods.summary: Picqer authenticates with HTTP Basic Auth using your API key. Per Picqer's docs, the API key is sent as the Basic Auth username and the password is ignored. In this connector, set the username config field to your Picqer API key; the password secret field maps to the Basic Auth password slot and is not required (Picqer ignores it). Set organization_name to your Picqer subdomain so requests target https://{organization_name}.picqer.com/api/v1/.

setupSteps.title: Create an API key in Picqer
setupSteps.body: In the Picqer web app, go to Settings > API Keys and click to add a new API key. Give it a descriptive name (a dedicated key per application is recommended for security), add the key, and copy the generated key string. You can revoke a key at any time from the same screen.

setupSteps.title: Find your organization subdomain
setupSteps.body: Your Picqer account is reached at a unique subdomain, for example example.picqer.com. Take the subdomain portion (the part before .picqer.com) and use it as the organization_name config field. The connector uses it to build the API base URL https://{organization_name}.picqer.com/api/v1/.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Set organization_name to your Picqer subdomain and username to the API key you created. Leave the password secret field empty unless your setup requires a placeholder, because Picqer ignores the Basic Auth password. Set start_date to the point from which the initial sync should begin.

sources.title: How to create API keys? - Picqer Support
